name: Pierre Macherey
summary: Pierre Macherey is represented here by a minimal backlog stub so routed on-disk source texts can surface in the Source tab pending fuller review.

engaged_works.title: Hegel O Spinoza Pierre Macherey
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Routed from the 2026-05-10 Tier-1 publication backlog as an on-disk source witness.
engaged_works.summary: This on-disk text is being wired into the Source tab as a citation witness for Pierre Macherey, Hegel O Spinoza Pierre Macherey. The entry is intentionally minimal: it records the routed file and basic bibliographic shape so the site can surface the source while fuller review waits. A later pass should tighten the title, edition, and passage coverage against the on-disk witness.

core_thesis: Macherey treats works not as organic unities expressing an author's intention but as productions whose necessity appears in their gaps, dissonances, and silences ([A Theory of Literary Production, Part I](cite://macherey/a-theory-of-literary-production/1); [Hegel or Spinoza, ch. 2](cite://macherey/hegel-or-spinoza/2)). Interpretation therefore asks what a text could not say within the ideological conditions of its making. This anti-expressivism is tied to a Spinozist preference for immanent causality over reconciled totality.
